name: Triple Chocolate Cheesecake
recipeIngredient: 1 1/4 cups chocolate wafer cookies, crushed fine
4 ounces milk chocolate, melted and cooled
4 ounces white chocolate, melted and cooled
4 ounces bitter chocolate or semisweet chocolate, melted and cooled
4 ounces bitter chocolate or semisweet chocolate, chopped
recipeInstructions:
Adapted from "A Capital Affair" by the Junior League of Harrisburg, Pa.
Crust: Preheat oven to 350°F. In a bowl, mix melted butter and crumbs. Press over bottom of 10-in. springform pan. Bake 8-10 minutes until set. Cool on rack. With unmelted butter, grease pan sides. Wrap outside of pan with heavy-duty foil; refrigerate. Reduce oven to 325°F.
Filling: In a large bowl with electric mixer on medium-high speed, beat cream cheese 2-3 minutes until fluffy. Gradually beat in sugar until smooth. With mixer on medium speed, beat in eggs, 1 at a time, just until incorporated. Beat in cream. Divide batter evenly among 3 medium bowls (about 2 1/2 cups each). Stir melted milk chocolate into one bowl, white chocolate into the next, and bittersweet chocolate into the last.
Pour milk chocolate batter into prepared crust in an even layer. Pour and spread white chocolate batter over milk chocolate layer; do not let batters run together. Top with bittersweet chocolate layer, carefully spreading evenly. Set springform pan in the center of a large roasting pan: place on oven rack. Pour boiling water into roasting pan to reach halfway up the springform pan. Bake 2 hours or until set. Turn off oven; let stand in oven 1 hour. Transfer springform pan to rack; let cool completely. Remove foil. Cover; refrigerate at least 8 hours or overnight.
Glaze: In a saucepan, heat cream to a gentle boil. Place chocolate in a bowl, add cream. Let stand 1 minute; whisk until smooth. Pour over top of cheesecake; spread to edges. Chill 15 minutes or until set. Run a thin-bladed knife around the edge of pan; carefully remove pan side. Transfer cake to serving plate. Let stand at room temperature for 1/2 hour. Garnish top edge of cake with toasted slice almonds, if desired. Slice cake with a large knife; between slices. Wipe blade clean and dip blade in warm water.
